title: 9 Herbal Tinctures For Premenstrual Syndrome
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful way to manage premenstrual syndrome (PMS) symptoms. These natural remedies work by interacting with the body's hormonal systems to reduce symptoms like mood swings, bloating, and cramps. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is an example of an herb that can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. This is because it contains compounds that slow down brain activity, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.  Vitex agnus-castus, or chasteberry, is another herb that's been used to treat PMS symptoms. It's believed to help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to PMS symptoms. Some studies have shown that chasteberry can reduce symptoms like mood swings, breast tenderness, and anxiety.  Paeonia lactiflora, or peony root, is also used to treat PMS symptoms. It's been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to calm the nervous system and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate symptoms like cramps, bloating, and mood swings.  Using herbal tinctures for PMS can bring a range of benefits to your life. By reducing symptoms like anxiety and mood swings, you may find it easier to manage daily tasks and relationships. You may also find that you're able to sleep better and have more energy. Additionally, using natural remedies like herbal tinctures can help you avoid the side effects of prescription medications, like drowsiness and stomach problems. To get the most out of herbal tinctures, it's best to talk to a healthcare professional about the best options for your specific needs. They can help you choose the right herbs and dosages to alleviate your PMS symptoms and improve your overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures contains valerenic acid, valepotriates, and isovaleric acid as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have a sedative and relaxing effect, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress associated with premenstrual syndrome. The valepotriates in Valeriana officinalis tinctures also have a mild antispasmodic effect, which can help to alleviate cramps and discomfort experienced during PMS. Additionally, the valerenic acid has a mild antidepressant effect, which can help to improve mood and reduce irritability. By reducing anxiety, stress, and cramps, Valeriana officinalis tinctures can help to provide relief from the symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Valeriana officinalis roots and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Chop the roots into small pieces and add them to the jar with the vodka, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and label it. Take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day as needed for PMS symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus tinctures contains iridoid glycosides, including aucubin and agnuside, which have a regulating effect on hormones. These compounds help to balance the body's production of estrogen and progesterone, leading to a reduction in symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture have a calming effect, which can help to alleviate anxiety and mood swings often experienced during this time. Additionally, the saponins in Vitex agnus-castus have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce physical symptoms such as breast tenderness and bloating. By regulating hormones and reducing inflammation, Vitex agnus-castus tinctures can help to alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
remedies.image_alt: Vitex agnus-castus herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers, 2 cups of vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers in the glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka over the Vitex agnus-castus mixture, making sure they are completely covered.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora tinctures contains salicylic acid, paeoniflorin, and paeonol as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate cramps, bloating, and mood swings associated with premenstrual syndrome. Salicylic acid, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause pain and discomfort during menstruation. Paeoniflorin and paeonol also have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that often accompany PMS symptoms. By addressing these underlying issues, Paeonia lactiflora tinctures may help provide relief from premenstrual syndrome symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Paeonia lactiflora herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Paeonia lactiflora root, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Chop the Paeonia lactiflora root into small pieces and put them in the glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80% vodka over the chopped root in the jar, making sure that the root is completely covered.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica tinctures contains bioactive constituents like ferulic acid and volatile oils that help alleviate premenstrual syndrome symptoms. Ferulic acid reduces inflammation, which is a key contributor to PMS symptoms like breast tenderness and mood swings. The volatile oils in angelica archangelica tincture, such as borneol and bornyl acetate, have anti-spasmodic properties that ease cramps and other physical discomforts associated with PMS. Angelica's tincture also contains phenolic compounds that help balance hormones and reduce anxiety, common issues for women experiencing PMS. By reducing inflammation, easing physical discomforts, and balancing hormones, angelica archangelica tinctures can help alleviate PMS symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of fresh Angelica archangelica roots, 1 cup of 80-proof vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Chop the Angelica archangelica roots into small pieces and fill the glass jar about 2/3 full.
Pour the vodka over the chopped roots, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures contains the bioactive constituents linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their calming and relaxing properties. These compounds help to soothe anxiety and stress, common symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS). The anti-inflammatory properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to reduce bloating and cramping associated with PMS. Additionally, the sedative properties of these compounds promote better sleep, which is often disrupted during the premenstrual phase. Regular use of Lavandula angustifolia tinctures may help to alleviate PMS symptoms and improve overall well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 750ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the dried flowers to the jar and cover them with vodka. Stir well to combine.
Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small glass dropper bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. Use 5-10 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures contains flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, and phenolic acids like hyperoside. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in the body. The tincture's flavonoids also have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that often accompany premenstrual syndrome. Additionally, the tincture contains hyperforin, a compound that has a sedative effect and helps regulate mood swings. By reducing inflammation, anxiety, and stress, Hypericum perforatum tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 750ml of 80% vodka.
Combine the flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place. Take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures contains bioactive constituents like avenacosides and avenanthramides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce symptoms of premenstrual syndrome. Avenacosides specifically target and calm the nervous system, alleviating anxiety and stress that often accompany PMS. Avenanthramides also have a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and helping with sleep disturbances. By regulating hormonal fluctuations and soothing the nervous system, Avena sativa tinctures can provide relief from PMS symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa roots and 1 cup of 80% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of dried Avena sativa roots to the jar and pour 1 cup of ethanol or vodka over the roots.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the roots release their active ingredients into the liquid.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to small glass dropper bottles and label them. The recommended dose is 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, 1-2 weeks before your period.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are bioactive constituents that help alleviate premenstrual syndrome symptoms. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation and ease cramps. Terpenoids, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, improve blood flow and relax muscles, which can help with bloating and breast tenderness. These compounds also have a sedative effect, which can help regulate mood swings and anxiety. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Ginkgo biloba tinctures may provide relief from premenstrual syndrome symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves, 1 cup of 80-proof vodka, and a glass jar with a lid.
Chop the Ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and place them in the glass jar.
Pour the 1 cup of vodka over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the jar, making sure they are fully covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a dark place for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are the active constituents that contribute to its effectiveness in relieving premenstrual syndrome symptoms. Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with PMS. The alkaloids, including harmine and harmaline, have a sedative effect, which can help calm anxiety and stress that often accompany PMS. Glycosides, such as passifloric acid, have a spasmolytic effect, which can help relax muscle cramps and spasms common in PMS. By combining these properties, Passiflora incarnata tinctures can help alleviate PMS symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and relief.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of dried Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers. Use a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried plant material with 1 cup of 80% vodka in the glass jar. Make sure the plant material is completely covered by the vodka.
Close the jar and shake it well for 10 seconds. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid tincture to a dropper bottle. Use 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed to relieve premenstrual syndrome symptoms.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease premenstrual syndrome symptoms. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have calming effects that can reduce anxiety and cramps. They also contain antioxidants that can help balance hormones. Some women find that drinking these teas regularly helps minimize their PMS symptoms, but it's not a cure for everyone.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to managing premenstrual syndrome symptoms. However, it's generally recommended to limit daily consumption to avoid potential interactions with medications or exacerbating underlying conditions. Drinking herbal teas every day may also cause the body to build up a tolerance, reducing their effectiveness.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with premenstrual syndrome symptoms, but it's hard to predict exactly when they'll take effect. Some people notice relief after a few days, while others might take a week or two to feel a difference. Everyone's body is different, so it may take some trial and error to find the right tea and timing for you.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea can be a great way to help with premenstrual syndrome. For many women, it's best to have a cup in the evening, around 30 minutes to an hour before bedtime. This can help calm your body and mind, making it easier to relax and fall asleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas may interact with prescription medications for premenstrual syndrome (PMS). Some herbs, like St. John's Wort, can affect how these medications work. This might make symptoms worse or change how medications are absorbed. If you're taking prescription medications for PMS, it's a good idea to check the ingredients of any herbal teas you're considering.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and have premenstrual syndrome (PMS), it's generally best to avoid herbal teas that can stimulate the uterus. Some teas, like raspberry leaf and red clover, can cause contractions, which might not be safe for the baby. However, peppermint and ginger teas are usually okay, as they're calming and don't affect the uterus.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful way to manage premenstrual syndrome while breastfeeding. However, some herbs like peppermint and ginger are usually safe to consume during breastfeeding. But it's best to avoid teas with high levels of caffeine, like yerba mate and guarana, as they can pass into breast milk and affect your baby.
title_2: Women with Endometriosis and Premenstrual Syndrome: 9 Herbal Tinctures to Try
